verification.note: Form applicability (who can/cannot use ITR-1/2/3/4) cross-checked against current AY 2026-27 ITR-form reporting and the CBDT-notified form changes. ITR-1 now allows Section 112A LTCG up to Rs 1.25 lakh when no capital loss is carried forward, but this tool conservatively routes detected capital-gains uploads to ITR-2 because broker uploads can include disqualifying STCG/loss details. Due dates are separated by profile: most non-audit individual/ITR-2 paths use 31-Jul-2026; non-audit business/professional ITR-3 paths use 31-Aug-2026; audit paths use 31-Oct-2026. CORRECTED/CLARIFIED 2026-07-06 CA review: the 31-Aug-2026 bucket is a genuinely new category for AY2026-27 (historically non-audit business/professional filers were also due 31-Jul), but per the department's own notification it specifically EXCLUDES an assessee (or a firm's partner) subject to Section 92E transfer-pricing reporting - those get 30-Nov-2026 instead, not 31-Aug. This tool does not yet carve out the Section 92E exception; a filer with international/specified-domestic transactions requiring a Section 92E report should confirm 30-Nov applies to them rather than 31-Aug. No general CBDT extension of the 31-Jul deadline had been announced for AY2026-27 as of this check, unlike AY2025-26 which was extended to 15/16-Sep-2025 - confirm on the e-filing portal before filing since this is time-sensitive and could still change.

business_or_speculative_non_audit.due_date_note: Excludes a filer (or firm partner) subject to Section 92E transfer-pricing reporting - see section_92e_due_date below.

section_92e_transfer_pricing.note: An assessee (or partner of a firm) required to furnish a Section 92E transfer-pricing report gets 30-Nov, not the 31-Aug non-audit business date or the 31-Oct audit date. Not yet applied by this tool - flagged for the CA to confirm before relying on the 31-Aug default for any filer with international or specified-domestic transactions.

itr1_disqualifiers: Any short-term capital gains, or any capital gains other than LTCG under Section 112A up to Rs 1.25 lakh
Any brought-forward or carried-forward loss under any income head, including a Section 112A capital loss
Income from more than one house property
Any income from business or profession
You were a director in a company at any time during the year
You held unlisted equity shares at any time during the year
Any foreign income, any foreign asset, or signing authority in an account outside India
Tax deducted under Section 194N (large cash withdrawals)
Tax on ESOPs deferred (eligible start-up ESOPs)
